sourceMovie.title: Boruto: Naruto the Movie
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Boruto: Naruto the Movie (2015) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Naruto Shippuden the Movie: The Will of Fire
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Boruto: Naruto the Movie clicked for you, Naruto Shippuden the Movie: The Will of Fire (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Fairy Tail: Dragon Cry
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Boruto: Naruto the Movie clicked for you, Fairy Tail: Dragon Cry (2017) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Boruto: Naruto the Movie?
faq.answer: Try Naruto Shippuden the Movie: The Will of Fire, Fairy Tail: Dragon Cry, One Piece: The Movie—each shares DNA with Boruto: Naruto the Movie while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Boruto: Naruto the Movie?
faq.answer: Boruto: Naruto the Movie (2015) is best described as Adventure, Action, Animation, Comedy, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Boruto: Naruto the Movie part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Naruto Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
